description.content: Upload a vulnerability report file for similarity checking and sloppiness scoring

The vulnerability report file (.txt, .md, .pdf). Either file or rawText must be provided.
Plain text content of the vulnerability report. Either file, rawText, or reportUrl must be provided.
HTTPS URL to a plain-text report (GitHub raw, Gist, GitLab, Pastebin, etc.). Auto-converts GitHub blob URLs to raw. Max 5MB.
Whether to show this report in the public recent reports feed (This can only be one of true,false)

Skip LLM analysis — use only local heuristic/statistical scoring. Sent as string in multipart form data. Forced to "true" server-side when skipRedaction is "true". (This can only be one of true,false)

item.name: Delete a previously submitted report

request.name: Delete a previously submitted report

description.content: Permanently deletes a report and all associated data. Requires the delete token returned at submission time.

item.name: Get verification badge data for a report

request.name: Get verification badge data for a report

description.content: Returns a lightweight verification summary for embedding in bug reports

item.name: Compare two reports side by side

request.name: Compare two reports side by side

description.content: Returns redacted text snippets of both reports for side-by-side similarity comparison

item.name: Get the chronological score evolution timeline for a report

request.name: Get the chronological score evolution timeline for a report

description.content:
Returns every recorded composite score for this report — the original
analysis plus each subsequent backfill rescore. Each entry carries the
composite score + tier + timestamp + correlation id. Per-engine
sub-scores are included only for the current scoring event (where
authentic engine numbers are persisted on the row); past entries
return `engines: null` because today's analysis_traces schema does
not persist per-engine numeric scores. `codeVersion` is reserved for
a future scorer-version field and is currently always null. When the
score-stability-monitor task lands a dedicated `report_rescore_log`
table this endpoint will switch to reading from it; today the
canonical per-row audit trail is the `rescoreHistory` array on the
engines blob. Hidden from the UI when only a single entry exists
(no rescores have happened) so fresh reports stay quiet.

item.name: Get exportable markdown triage report

request.name: Get exportable markdown triage report

description.content: Returns a formatted markdown summary with score, verification results, evidence, recommendation, and challenge questions — ready to paste into Jira/ServiceNow

item.name: Check a report against the database without storing it

request.name: Check a report against the database without storing it

description.content: For report receivers -- analyze a report for similarity and sloppiness without adding it to the database

formdata.description: Plain text content of the vulnerability report
HTTPS URL to a plain-text report (GitHub raw, Gist, GitLab, Pastebin, etc.). Auto-converts GitHub blob URLs to raw. Max 5MB.

item.name: Look up a report by content hash

request.name: Look up a report by content hash

description.content: Check if a report with the given SHA-256 content hash has been seen before

item.name: Get recent public reports

request.name: Get recent public reports

description.content: Returns recent reports that submitters opted to show in the public feed

query.description: Filter by slop tier name (exact match)
Filter by cached AVRI rubric family id (exact match against reports.avri_family).

Filter the feed to AVRI Engine 2 fabricated-evidence cohorts. `fake_raw_http`
keeps only rows where signalBreakdown.avri.rawHttp.isFake is true,
`stripped_trace` keeps only rows where signalBreakdown.avri.crashTrace.isStripped
is true, and `either` keeps rows that match either flag. Mirrors the
fakeRawHttp / strippedCrashTrace booleans surfaced on each feed row.

item.name: feedback

description.content: User feedback

item.name: Get a proof-of-work challenge for feedback submission

request.name: Get a proof-of-work challenge for feedback submission

description.content: Returns a SHA-256 proof-of-work challenge that must be solved before submitting feedback. This prevents automated spam.

item.name: Submit user feedback about the tool

request.name: Submit user feedback about the tool

description.content: Allows users to share whether the tool was helpful and suggest improvements. Requires a solved proof-of-work challenge.

item.name: Get aggregated feedback analytics

request.name: Get aggregated feedback analytics

description.content: Returns summary stats, rating distribution, daily trends, score-vs-feedback correlation, outlier reports, and recent feedback entries

item.name: Honest precision/recall computed from the locked holdout split (Task

request.name: Honest precision/recall computed from the locked holdout split (Task

description.content:
Returns binary-classifier precision/recall/F1/accuracy computed
ONLY from the deterministic 20% feedback holdout that calibration
suggestions never touch, alongside the same numbers computed from
the in-sample 80%. Treats the engine as a binary "slop" classifier
where `predicted slop = slopScore >= tierThresholds.high` and
`actually slop = rating <= 2 OR helpful = false`. Each partition
returns nullable precision/recall/F1/accuracy so the UI can render
"insufficient data" instead of a misleading 0 when a partition is
empty.

item.name: Get calibration report with tuning suggestions

request.name: Get calibration report with tuning suggestions

description.content: Analyzes feedback data against scoring results to suggest weight and threshold adjustments. Uses volume gating to ensure suggestions are based on sufficient data.

item.name: Get rolling AVRI calibration drift report

request.name: Get rolling AVRI calibration drift report

description.content:
Rolling weekly view of the AVRI composite for production reports,
bucketed by triage outcome (T1-equivalent vs T3-equivalent). Surfaces
per-family means and drift flags (rubric collapse and per-family
weight drift) so reviewers can spot when the AVRI rubric needs
re-tuning.

query.description: How many weeks of history to scan (default 8, capped at 26).

item.name: List the persisted AVRI drift notification dedup state

request.name: List the persisted AVRI drift notification dedup state

description.content:
Task #196 — Returns the per-flag dedup records that have already been
dispatched via `POST /feedback/calibration/avri-drift/notify` (or the
in-process auto-trigger). Each entry would silently suppress its
corresponding flag on the next dispatch run unless re-armed via
`/feedback/calibration/avri-drift/notifications/rearm`. The endpoint
is auth-gated (strict) because the records include the original flag
`detail` string.

item.name: Re-arm one or more previously-notified AVRI drift flags

request.name: Re-arm one or more previously-notified AVRI drift flags

description.content:
Task #196 — Removes one or more entries from the AVRI drift
notification dedup state by `key` so the next dispatch run treats
the matching flags as never-seen and re-fires the webhook. Useful
for the "acknowledged-but-not-fixed by the fix-by date" workflow,
where reviewers want a flag re-paged after the fix window lapses
without hand-editing `data/avri-drift-notifications.json`.

Unknown keys are reported back via `notFound` (mirroring the
per-phrase removal pattern); the request returns 200 when at least
one entry was re-armed and 404 only when nothing matched.

The optional `reviewer` and `rationale` body fields feed the
bounded re-arm audit log persisted alongside the dedup state
so multi-reviewer teams can see who re-armed which flag and why.

response.name: At least one entry was re-armed
No entries matched any of the supplied keys

item.name: Get per-replica AVRI drift scheduler status

request.name: Get per-replica AVRI drift scheduler status

description.content:
Operator-visible status of the AVRI drift-notification scheduler
across every replica that has registered a heartbeat. Backs the
calibration page's per-replica heartbeat table so reviewers can
confirm every replica's timer is firing — and spot a wedged
replica hiding behind a healthy one — without scraping logs.

Unauthenticated, matching `/feedback/calibration/auth-status`:
the response is timestamps + booleans + replica identity only
(no error text, webhook URL, or token).

The response is an array because the API server runs in a
multi-replica deploy. Each entry is keyed by `replicaId` (a
stable per-process identity, e.g. `${hostname}-${bootHex}`) so
the calibration UI can render one row per replica. The replica
that handled the request returns a live in-memory snapshot;
peer replicas return their last-persisted heartbeat from the
shared `data/avri-drift-notifications.json` state file.

response.name: Per-replica scheduler status snapshots
response.body:
[
  {
    "replicaId": "string",
    "hostname": "string",
    "heartbeatAt": "1956-11-08T07:16:28.282Z",
    "schedulerStarted": false,
    "startedAt": "1970-03-26T23:05:14.750Z",
    "intervalMs": 1981,
    "retryIntervalMs": 4157,

item.name: Score-stability tier-flip summary (reviewer-only, Task

request.name: Score-stability tier-flip summary (reviewer-only, Task

description.content:
Reviewer-only summary of the nightly score-stability monitor.
Aggregates `report_rescore_log` rows from the last 7 days into
per-day flip counts broken down by direction (legit→slop,
slop→legit, tightened, loosened, lateral) plus the day's
overall flip-rate. Backs the tier-flip chart on
`/feedback-analytics`. Strict-auth: an elevated flip-rate is a
leading indicator of an active scoring regression, same access
policy as the other reviewer-only drift surfaces.

item.name: Per-replica score-stability scheduler heartbeat (Task

request.name: Per-replica score-stability scheduler heartbeat (Task

description.content:
Operator-visible status of the in-process score-stability
scheduler for the responding replica. Mirrors the AVRI drift /
rescore-backfill scheduler-status endpoints: timestamps +
booleans + small numeric counters only, so the endpoint stays
safe to expose unauthenticated alongside the other heartbeat
surfaces.

item.name: List the persisted AVRI drift re-arm audit log

request.name: List the persisted AVRI drift re-arm audit log

description.content:
Returns the bounded audit log of every reviewer-driven re-arm
event recorded by
`POST /feedback/calibration/avri-drift/notifications/rearm`. The
log is capped at 200 entries (oldest trimmed first). Strict-auth
because the entries include the original flag `detail` plus
reviewer/rationale context.

item.name: List recent calibration auth brute-force alert decisions

request.name: List recent calibration auth brute-force alert decisions

description.content:
Task #399 — Bounded snapshot of the in-process ring buffer of
dispatched calibration-auth brute-force alerts so reviewers can
confirm at a glance whether the latest alert was them
fat-fingering a token or a real probe, without round-tripping
through pino logs.

The entries mirror the webhook payload (sans the constant
`event` discriminator and the static `recommendedActions`
runbook copy). The buffer is in-memory only — restarts clear it
and a multi-replica deploy reflects whichever replica handled
the request. Strict-auth because the entries include client IP
plus per-route metadata.

query.description:
Maximum number of recent alerts to return (newest first).
Defaults to 10 and is clamped server-side to the buffer
size (50). Values that fail to parse as a positive integer
return 400.

item.name: Get current and historical scoring configurations

request.name: Get current and historical scoring configurations

description.content: Returns the active scoring config version and full version history.

item.name: Probe whether the reviewer token is configured/valid

request.name: Probe whether the reviewer token is configured/valid

description.content:
Task #117 — Returns boolean signals describing whether the API server
requires a reviewer token for calibration mutations and whether the
caller's request would be accepted by `requireCalibrationAuth`. This
endpoint is intentionally NOT auth-gated so the dashboard can render
a "Reviewer token: configured / missing / invalid" indicator BEFORE
the reviewer triggers a mutation that 401s. The configured token is
never echoed in the response — only boolean signals.

item.name: Apply calibration changes to scoring config

request.name: Apply calibration changes to scoring config

description.content: Creates a new scoring configuration version with the specified changes. All subsequent reports will be scored with the new config.

item.name: List the curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrases

request.name: List the curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrases

description.content:
Returns the active list of FLAT-family hand-wavy marker phrases used by
AVRI Engine 2 to apply the slop haircut. Reviewers can extend this list
through POST/DELETE without an engineer redeploying the service.

response.name: Active hand-wavy phrase list

item.name: Append a new FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

request.name: Append a new FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

description.content:
Appends a phrase to the curated FLAT hand-wavy marker list. Phrases are
normalized to lowercase + collapsed whitespace before storage. Subsequent
triages pick the new phrase up immediately — no redeploy required.

response.name: Phrase already present (no change)
Phrase appended

item.name: Edit a curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase in place

request.name: Edit a curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase in place

description.content:
Updates the `category`, `rationale`, and/or the phrase string itself
of an existing curated marker without losing the original add audit
context. Each edit appends a `HandwavyEditEntry` to the marker's
`edits` log recording who made the change, when, and the
before/after for any field that actually changed.

Task #247 — when `newPhrase` is provided AND its normalized form
(lowercase + collapsed whitespace) differs from the existing
phrase, the marker's row identity is rewritten in place to the new
phrase. The marker keeps its original `addedBy`/`addedAt`/
`rationale` and the rename is recorded as a `phrase` before/after
change on the appended audit entry. Renaming to a normalized form
already used by another active phrase is rejected so the active
list stays a set.

response.name: Edit applied (or no-op if updates matched the existing values)
Invalid request body or update values

item.name: Remove one or many FLAT hand-wavy marker phrases

request.name: Remove one or many FLAT hand-wavy marker phrases

description.content:
Removes a phrase (matched after lowercase + whitespace normalization)
from the curated FLAT hand-wavy marker list. Task #135 — accepts
either a single phrase (`{phrase}`) or a batch (`{phrases: [...]}`)
in one round-trip; the batch path appends one history entry that
lists every removed phrase instead of N separate entries.

Task #145 — when the batch body has `dryRun: true`, the server
returns a preview (corpus impact + per-phrase outcomes) and does
NOT mutate the active list, history, or cache.

Task #155 — the single-phrase body also accepts `dryRun: true`
and returns the same corpus + production removal-impact summary
(with `batch: false`) so the in-UI Trash flow can warn reviewers
before a one-click removal lands.

response.name:
Phrase removed (single path), batch processed and at least one
phrase was removed (batch path; see `batch: true` and `results`),
single-phrase dry-run preview (`dryRun: true`, `batch: false`),
OR batch dry-run preview (`dryRun: true`, `batch: true`).

Phrase not in the list (single path) OR batch matched zero phrases
(batch path returns the same `results` shape as 200).

item.name: List recent FLAT hand-wavy phrase BATCH removal entries (picker-friendly summary)

request.name: List recent FLAT hand-wavy phrase BATCH removal entries (picker-friendly summary)

description.content:
Task #160 — Slim summary of the most recent batch removal entries from
the hand-wavy phrase removal-history log, intended for the
reinstate-batch CLI's interactive picker. Single-phrase removal
entries are omitted because they're not reinstatable through the
/reinstate-batch endpoint. Newest entries are returned first; default
page size is 10, capped at 50 via the `limit` query parameter.

query.description: Maximum number of batch entries to return (newest first). Defaults to 10, capped at 50.

item.name: Fetch the FULL inner phrase list for a single batch removal entry

request.name: Fetch the FULL inner phrase list for a single batch removal entry

description.content:
Task #176 — Sibling detail endpoint for the picker list above. Returns
every inner phrase from one batch removal entry (not just the
5-phrase sample the list endpoint surfaces) plus the per-phrase
audit metadata (category, original adder, per-phrase reinstated
flag). The reinstate-batch CLI calls this to render a "preview"
step between the picker / `--removed-at` flag and the final
confirmation prompt; Task #244 wires the same endpoint into the
web reviewer's picker so reviewers see every phrase before
confirming.

Single-phrase removal entries return 404 with `reason: "not-a-batch"`
— they are not reinstatable through /reinstate-batch and the caller
should fall back to /reinstate.

variable.description: (Required) ISO 8601 timestamp of the parent batch removal entry. Must be URL-encoded by the caller.

response.name: Full inner phrase list with per-phrase audit metadata

variable.description: (Required) ISO 8601 timestamp of the parent batch removal entry. Must be URL-encoded by the caller.

response.name: Missing or empty removedAt path parameter

variable.description: (Required) ISO 8601 timestamp of the parent batch removal entry. Must be URL-encoded by the caller.

response.name:
No removal-history entry matches the supplied removedAt
(`reason: "history-not-found"`) or the matched entry is a
single-phrase removal that's not addressable through this
endpoint (`reason: "not-a-batch"`).

variable.description: (Required) ISO 8601 timestamp of the parent batch removal entry. Must be URL-encoded by the caller.

item.name: Undo a brand-new add of a FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

request.name: Undo a brand-new add of a FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

description.content:
Task #130 — mirror of /reinstate. A reviewer who just added a phrase
can press Undo within a short server-side window (default 5 minutes)
and the marker is removed. The resulting history row is tagged
`undone: true` so the audit trail clearly records "added then undone"
rather than producing an unrelated manual-removal entry. The active
marker is matched by `phrase` + `addedAt`.

response.name: Phrase undone
No matching active marker (or curated default)
addedAt mismatch or undo window expired

item.name: Undo every still-in-window FLAT hand-wavy phrase add in one round-trip

request.name: Undo every still-in-window FLAT hand-wavy phrase add in one round-trip

description.content:
Task #233 — bulk wrapper around POST /feedback/calibration/handwavy-phrases/undo.
The reviewer's UI tracks every reviewer-added phrase that is still
inside its per-marker undo window (default 5 minutes) and pressing
"Undo last N adds" sends every `(phrase, addedAt)` pair in one
round-trip. The server walks each entry through the per-marker
undo path so each successful undo still appends its own
`undone: true` history row — the audit contract is NOT collapsed
into a single batch row, so per-phrase provenance is preserved
for downstream tooling. Per-entry failures (`not-found`,
`addedAt-mismatch`, `window-expired`, `no-addedAt`) are reported
in the per-phrase `results` array instead of failing the whole
call so a reviewer who fires the button right as one entry's
window elapses still gets the rest rolled back.

response.name: Batch processed (see `undoneCount` and per-phrase `results`)
Invalid body (missing / malformed `entries`, oversized batch, or non-string `reviewer`)

item.name: Reinstate every not-yet-reinstated phrase from a single batch removal entry

request.name: Reinstate every not-yet-reinstated phrase from a single batch removal entry

description.content:
Task #144 — single-round-trip reinstate of every inner phrase from one
Task #135 batch removal entry. The reviewer supplies the parent
entry's `removedAt`; the server reinstates each remaining inner
phrase, records the current reviewer on each one, and flips the
aggregate `reinstated` flag once everything is back. Inner phrases
that have already been reinstated (partial undo earlier) or that
have been re-added to the active list separately are skipped with a
reason instead of failing the whole call. Per-phrase reinstate via
the existing /reinstate endpoint still works for partial undos.

Task #159 — when `dryRun: true` is sent in the body, the server
returns the same `results` / `reinstatedCount` / `skipped` shape
(with `dryRun: true` and `batch: true` flags) without mutating
the active marker list or appending to the removal-history log.
Reviewers can preview exactly which inner phrases would be
reinstated and which would be skipped before pressing yes.
Mirrors the dry-run pattern used by the bulk-remove endpoint:
both the mutating and dry-run paths return HTTP 200 and callers
discriminate on the `dryRun` flag in the body.

response.name:
Batch processed (mutating path; see `reinstatedCount` and
`results`) OR dry-run preview when `dryRun: true` was sent
(`dryRun: true` and `batch: true` flags, no state was mutated).
Invalid body (missing or non-string `removedAt`/`reviewer`, or non-boolean `dryRun`).
No matching removal-history entry for that removedAt.
The matched history entry exists but is a single-phrase removal (use /reinstate instead).

item.name: Reinstate a previously removed FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase from history

request.name: Reinstate a previously removed FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase from history

description.content:
Task #121 — re-adds a phrase straight from the removal-history log so a
reviewer doesn't have to retype it (and its rationale) into the add
form. The history entry is matched by `phrase` + `removedAt`. The
marker is added with the original category and rationale; the CURRENT
reviewer is recorded as `addedBy` and a fresh `addedAt` timestamp is
used so the audit trail still shows who reinstated and when. The
history row is then flagged `reinstated: true` so the same row cannot
be reinstated twice.

response.name: Phrase reinstated
Already reinstated or already in the active list

item.name: Revert a single edit on a curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

request.name: Revert a single edit on a curated FLAT hand-wavy marker phrase

description.content:
Task #132 — undoes a single entry from a curated phrase's `edits` log
in one click. The reviewer supplies the phrase and the `editedAt`
timestamp of the edit they want to undo; the server restores
whatever fields that entry recorded a change to (category and/or
rationale) back to their `from` values, leaving any field the entry
did NOT change alone. The audit log stays append-only — the revert
appears as a fresh `HandwavyEditEntry` with the inverse before/after
pair, attributed to the reviewer who pressed the button. A no-op
revert (current values already match the target) returns
`edited: false` and does not append a new entry.

response.name: Revert applied (or no-op when current values already match)
Phrase or matching edit entry not found

item.name: stats

description.content: Platform statistics

item.name: Get platform statistics

request.name: Get platform statistics

description.content: Returns aggregate statistics for the platform

item.name: Get recent submission activity

request.name: Get recent submission activity

description.content: Returns a summary of recent report submissions

item.name: Get slop score distribution

request.name: Get slop score distribution

description.content: Returns the distribution of slop scores across all reports

item.name: Record a page visit

request.name: Record a page visit

description.content: Records a privacy-respecting unique visitor count using hashed identifiers

item.name: Get visitor statistics

request.name: Get visitor statistics

description.content: Returns total unique visitors and total visits

item.name: Get aggregate corpus statistics

request.name: Get aggregate corpus statistics

description.content:
Returns at-a-glance aggregates over the platform's full scoring corpus —
total reports, breakdown by tier, top 10 most-fired evidence signals,
top 10 CWE families, and a daily volume time series. All values are
already-aggregated counts; no individual report content is exposed.

item.name: Get 24h scoring latency snapshot

request.name: Get 24h scoring latency snapshot

description.content:
Public p50/p95/p99 latency for the end-to-end scoring pipeline plus a
per-engine breakdown, computed from the last 24h of analysis_traces.
Returns the histogram bins for the full pipeline and each engine, the
percentile values, and an optional worst-engine call-out when one
engine is dragging the tail (its p95 is more than 1.5x the median
engine p95 and contributes >25% of pipeline p95).

item.name: Get trend data over time

request.name: Get trend data over time

description.content: Returns daily report volume, tier breakdown, average score, and feedback trends for the specified time window

query.description: Number of days to look back

item.name: newsletter

description.content: Community newsletter signup

item.name: Subscribe to the VulnRap community mailing list

request.name: Subscribe to the VulnRap community mailing list

description.content:
Stores a SHA-256 HMAC of the email address (keyed with VISITOR_HMAC_KEY)
so duplicate signups can be detected without persisting the raw address.
When NEWSLETTER_FORWARD_URL is configured, the raw email is forwarded to
that destination as a best-effort POST. Per-IP rate limited to 20
signups per hour.

response.name: Subscription accepted (or already on the list)
Too many signups from this IP

item.name: cohort

description.content: Cohort baseline distributions for contextualizing scores

item.name: Get cohort baseline distribution

request.name: Get cohort baseline distribution

description.content:
Returns a 10-bucket histogram of VulnRap composite scores submitted in
the last 7 days plus the median score for that cohort. The composite
score is the same metric shown in the big number on the results page
header, so the percentile derived from these bins is a valid
percentile for that displayed score. When `cwe` is supplied, the
histogram and median are restricted to reports whose cached AVRI rubric
family matches the requested CWE family — otherwise the platform-wide
cohort is returned. Reports with no cached composite (legacy rows from
before the multi-engine consensus) are excluded from the cohort.

query.description:
Optional cached AVRI rubric family id (e.g. INJECTION,
MEMORY_CORRUPTION) to restrict the cohort. Unknown values fall
through to the platform-wide cohort.

item.name: public

description.content: Public-safe transparency endpoints (no reviewer-only fields)

item.name: Public-safe AVRI drift summary for the transparency page

request.name: Public-safe AVRI drift summary for the transparency page

description.content:
Task #617 — Public, read-only summary of the rolling weekly
T1-vs-T3 mean composite spread. Wraps the internal AVRI drift
compute and strips every reviewer-only field (cohort sample IDs,
per-family means, flag detail strings, thresholds,
bucketing-note, runbook path, raw bucket counts) so the
`/transparency` page can show that the platform actively monitors
itself for calibration drift without exposing internal triage
signals.

Returns up to the last 12 eligible weeks of `{ weekStart,
spread }` data points plus a current-vs-previous-week delta. If
no weekly aggregate has been computed yet, `weeks` is `[]` and
the current/previous/delta fields are `null` so the widget can
render an empty state.

item.name: presets

description.content: Curated public preset library (sensitivity + per-engine weights)

item.name: List curated scoring presets

request.name: List curated scoring presets

description.content:
Task #631 — Returns the curated preset library (sensitivity profiles
plus per-engine weights). Sourced from a static JSON file shipped
with the api-server; safe to cache for an hour. Each preset has an
opaque `id` that the `/check` page accepts via `?preset=<id>` to
load the configuration into local settings.

item.name: phrase-suggestions

description.content: User-suggested handwavy / ai-self-disclosure phrases (Task

item.name: Submit a user-suggested handwavy / ai-self-disclosure phrase

request.name: Submit a user-suggested handwavy / ai-self-disclosure phrase

description.content:
Task #634 — Public endpoint that lets anonymous end users propose new
phrases for reviewer triage. Submissions are queued (status="pending")
and never auto-applied. Rate-limited to 5 successful submissions per
IP per 24 hours; same-IP duplicates of the same normalized text in
the last 24h return 200 with `duplicate: true`.

response.name: Duplicate submission (already queued in the last 24h)

item.name: List queued user-suggested phrases (reviewer-only)

request.name: List queued user-suggested phrases (reviewer-only)

description.content:
Task #634 — Returns the suggestion queue for reviewer triage.
Defaults to status=pending. Requires the calibration token.

item.name: Mark a suggestion approved or rejected (reviewer-only)

request.name: Mark a suggestion approved or rejected (reviewer-only)

description.content:
Task #634 — Updates the status of a queued suggestion. The actual
addition to the active phrase list happens via the existing
/feedback/calibration/handwavy-phrases (or
/feedback/calibration/ai-self-disclosure-phrases) endpoints; this
is just the bookkeeping flip.

item.name: internal

description.content: Reviewer-only internal surfaces (gated by CALIBRATION_TOKEN)

item.name: Shadow scoring drift report (reviewer-only, Task

request.name: Shadow scoring drift report (reviewer-only, Task

description.content:
Reviewer-only listing of production reports whose shadow score
diverges from the live score by ≥1 tier or ≥10 score points
within the lookback window. Backs the "Shadow drift" tab on
`/feedback-analytics`. Strict-auth: shadow drift is a leading
signal of an in-flight scoring regression, same access policy
as the other reviewer-only drift surfaces.

item.name: audit

description.content: Reviewer audit log of mutation endpoints

item.name: List recorded reviewer mutations

request.name: List recorded reviewer mutations

description.content:
Task #645 — Reviewer-only paginated read of the `audit_log` table
populated by the audit-log middleware on every mutation route.
Strict reviewer auth required (sends reviewer identities and IPs).

query.description: Substring filter (case-insensitive) on the endpoint URL.

item.name: gallery

description.content: Curated sample-report gallery for new users

item.name: List curated sample reports

request.name: List curated sample reports

description.content:
Task #647 — Returns the curated sample-report gallery rendered on
`/gallery`. Sourced from a static JSON file shipped with the
api-server; safe to cache for an hour. Each sample carries the
display fields the gallery card needs (title, snippet, score,
top signals, label) plus a `reportId` pointing at a seed report
so click-through resolves on `/results/:id`.

variable.description: Base URL for the VulnRap API. Override to point at a self-hosted instance.

info.name: VulnRap API
info.description: VulnRap.com — Vulnerability Report Validation Platform. Auto-generated from lib/api-spec/openapi.yaml by scripts/generate-postman.mjs. Do not edit by hand — re-run the generator instead.
